draft.introduction: Matthew 21:1–11 records the triumphal entry into Jerusalem, with Jesus riding on a donkey in deliberate fulfillment of Zechariah 9:9. From Codex Sinaiticus, fourth century.

translation.text: This took place to fulfill what was spoken by the prophet, saying, “Say to the daughter of Zion: Behold, your king is coming to you, humble and mounted on a donkey, on a colt, the foal of a beast of burden.”
translation.original_text: τουτο δε γεγονεν ινα πληρωθη το ρηθεν δια του προφητου

translation.text: And the crowds that went before him and that followed him were shouting, “Hosanna to the Son of David! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest!” And when he entered Jerusalem, the whole city was stirred up, saying, “Who is this?”
translation.original_text: οι δε οχλοι οι προαγοντες αυτον και οι ακολουθουντες

translator_notes.note: Matthew’s quotation of Zechariah 9:9 presents the king as praus (πραὙς), “gentle” or “meek”—the same word used in the Beatitudes (5:5). The entry enacts a kingship defined by humility rather than force.
